query: What are the major phases, decision points and associated fees, if any, in the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process?
final_answer:
Here is a comprehensive breakdown of the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process, covering every major phase, each decision point, and all associated fees and financial obligations.

---

## NYISO Generation Interconnection Cluster Study Process

*Governing tariff: **OATT Attachment HH – Standard Interconnection Procedures**, effective May 2, 2024. Sources: [NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.2](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%252040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892); [Cluster Study Process Overview (Dec. 14, 2023)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/41716870/07b_NYISO%20Cluster%20Study%20Process%20Overview%20Figure_OC_30231214.pdf/32e9bee3-0b04-c9d1-9fbe-8ca4e70d614e); [Transition Cluster Technical Bulletin](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b); [NYISO IITF Compliance Plan (Nov. 2, 2023)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/40995296/Order%20No.%202023%20Compliance%20Plan_IITF_20231102_final%20version.pdf/8f96f9cb-e89b-73b5-121e-ce42bd11fa6a); [Notice of Transition Cluster Application Window (July 1, 2024)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1396587/Notice-of-the-Transition-Cluster-Study-Application-Window.pdf/ebbdb6b8-5cdd-f9fc-db90-adf2a107bb0d).*

---

### Overview

The cluster study process replaced the prior sequential (queue-by-queue) study process and is designed to evaluate groups ("clusters") of interconnection requests together on a recurring, roughly annual basis. The total end-to-end timeline for a standard cluster is approximately **569 calendar days (~1.6 years)**. The process is organized into the phases below.

---

## PHASE 0 — Pre-Application (Optional, Rolling)

**What it is:** An optional, rolling information-gathering phase that allows prospective developers to engage with NYISO and the Connecting Transmission Owner (CTO) *before* formally entering the queue. Available beginning May 2, 2024.

**What happens:**
- Developers may review the publicly posted **Heatmap** (shows estimated upgrade costs at candidate Points of Interconnection, or POIs) at any time at no cost.
- Developers may submit a formal **Pre-Application Request** for detailed POI-level information (line/substation ratings, constraints, known feasibility issues, planned upgrades, etc.).
- NYISO routes the request to the CTO, which holds a scoping meeting and issues a **Pre-Application Report within 25 business days** of that meeting.
- Maximum of **2 POIs per Pre-Application Request**.
- Submissions are *paused* during a 90-day blackout period (45 calendar days before an Application Window opens through the end of the Application Window), to let NYISO/CTOs focus on validating incoming Interconnection Requests.

**Fees:**
| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Pre-Application Request fee | **$5,000 per POI** (25% to NYISO, 75% to CTO/Affected TOs) |

---

## PHASE 1 — Application Window

**Duration:** 45 calendar days (standard) | 75 calendar days (Transition Cluster)

**What happens:**
- Interconnection Customers (ICs) submit Interconnection Requests (IRs) or CRIS-Only Requests via the NYISO Interconnection Projects Portal.
- NYISO validates submissions on a rolling basis (within **10 business days** of receipt; 15 BDs for Transition Cluster).
- ICs must cure any identified deficiencies within **10 business days** (15 BDs for Transition).
- NYISO identifies the Connecting Transmission Owner and any Affected Transmission Owners.
- A **Cluster Study Agreement** is circulated and must be executed by all parties within 10 calendar days.
- Projects may request a **limited POI change within 5 business days** of the cluster member list publication.

**Required submission materials:**
1. Completed IR (technical data, project models, conceptual one-line diagram, project layout)
2. Demonstration of **full site control** with officer attestation
3. IBR attestation (NYSRC Reliability Rule B.5 / IEEE 2800-2022) for projects >20 MW
4. Application Fee (cash only, **non-refundable**)
5. Study Deposit (cash, Letter of Credit, or surety bond)

**Fees:**

| Item | Standard IR | CRIS-Only Request |
|---|---|---|
| **Application Fee** (non-refundable, cash) | **$10,000** | **$5,000** |
| **Study Deposit** — facility < 80 MW | **$100,000** | — |
| **Study Deposit** — facility ≥ 80 MW and < 200 MW | **$150,000** | — |
| **Study Deposit** — facility ≥ 200 MW | **$250,000** | — |
| **Study Deposit** — CRIS-only | — | **$50,000** |

> **Study Deposit mechanics:** NYISO invoices developers for **actual study costs on a monthly basis**; the Study Deposit is only drawn upon if a developer fails to pay its monthly invoice. The Study Deposit remains in place throughout the entire Cluster Study Process. If a developer completes Phase 2 and accepts the cost allocation + posts Security, the **Study Deposit is refunded in full**.

---

## PHASE 2 — Customer Engagement Window (CEW)

**Duration:** 70 calendar days (standard) | 90 calendar days (Transition Cluster)

**What happens:**
- NYISO publishes the **full list of all Cluster Study Projects** within 10 business days of the Application Window close.
- The CTO conducts a **Physical Infeasibility Screen** to identify known issues precluding interconnection at the proposed POI.
- NYISO holds a **group Scoping Meeting** covering all cluster members.
- Projects identified as **physically infeasible** may not proceed unless the infeasibility is resolved.

---

## ⛳ DECISION POINT 1 — Phase 1 Entry Decision Period

**Duration:** 5 business days (after the CEW closes)

**The choice:** Proceed to Phase 1 Study, or withdraw.

**To proceed:** Post **Readiness Deposit 1 (RD1)** = **$4,000/MW** (not applicable for CRIS-only projects).

**Withdrawal Penalty (WP1):** If a project withdraws at or before this decision point:
- **Standard:** Forfeit **25% of the Study Deposit**
- **If physically infeasible:** Forfeit only **$5,000**
- **If withdrawn within 5 business days of cluster member list posting:** **$0 penalty**

---

## PHASE 3 — Phase 1 Study

**Duration:** 180 calendar days (standard) | 190 calendar days (Transition Cluster)
*(Deadline is measured from Phase 1 Study Start Date to NYISO Operating Committee approval of the Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report)*

**What happens:**
- NYISO finalizes the **Existing System Representation** (the baseline power flow model of NYISO's grid).
- The CTOs and Affected Transmission Owners (ATOs) study each project and identify:
  - **Connecting Transmission Owner's Attachment Facilities (CTOAFs):** The physical equipment connecting the new generator to the grid.
  - **Distribution Upgrades:** Required distribution-level network enhancements.
  - **Local System Upgrade Facilities (Local SUFs):** Transmission upgrades in the immediate vicinity of the POI needed for reliable interconnection.
- Cost estimates and preliminary construction schedules are produced for each project.
- Pre-work for Phase 2 (MIS/DIS analyses) is initiated.
- Phase 1 concludes with the **NYISO Operating Committee's approval of the Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report** (a public document).

---

## ⛳ DECISION POINT 2 — Phase 2 Entry Decision Period

**Duration:** 10 business days (after Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report is approved)

**The choice:** Proceed to Phase 2 Study, or withdraw.

**To proceed:** Post **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)**, which equals the **greater of:**
1. RD1 ($4,000/MW), OR
2. **20% of the Phase 1 cost estimate** for Local SUFs and CTOAFs

*(RD2 replaces RD1; deposits are cumulative/non-additive — excess is credited)*

**Withdrawal Penalty (WP2):** If a project withdraws at or before this decision point:
- **Standard:** Forfeit **50% of the Study Deposit + 10% of RD1**
- **If physically infeasible:** Forfeit only **25% of the Study Deposit**

---

## PHASE 4 — Phase 2 Study

**Duration:** 270 calendar days
*(Measured from Phase 2 Study Start Date to NYISO Operating Committee approval of the Cluster Study Report)*

**What happens:**
- NYISO builds and runs the **Cluster Baseline Assessment (CBA)** and **Cluster Project Assessment (CPA)** power flow base cases — full network models with all cluster projects simultaneously.
- For **ERIS (Energy Resource Interconnection Service)** requests: NYISO identifies and cost-estimates all **System Upgrade Facilities (SUFs)** — network-wide transmission upgrades required for reliable interconnection under the NYISO Minimum Interconnection Standard.
- For **CRIS (Capacity Resource Interconnection Service)** requests: NYISO conducts a **Cluster Study Deliverability Study** to assess whether projects can deliver their full capacity under the NYISO Deliverability Interconnection Standard, identifying any required **System Deliverability Upgrades (SDUs)**.
- CTOs / ATOs update Phase 1 cost estimates as needed.
- If warranted: an **Additional SDU Study** is conducted (iterative, as applicable), followed by an **Additional SDU Study Decision Period**.
- Phase 2 concludes with the **NYISO Operating Committee's approval of the Cluster Study Report** (the full study with costs allocated across all cluster projects).

---

## ⛳ DECISION POINT 3 — Final Decision Period (Iterative)

**Structure:** Multiple iterative rounds (each round is 7 calendar days); the number of rounds depends on how many projects accept or decline at each step.

**The choice:** Accept the **Project Cost Allocation** and pay cash or post **Security** for all identified upgrade facilities assigned to your project, or withdraw.

**Security amount:** **100% of the cost estimate** of all SUFs, SDUs, CTOAFs, and Distribution Upgrades identified for the project across Phase 1 and Phase 2.

**Withdrawal Penalty (WP3):** If a project withdraws at or before this final decision point:
- **Standard:** Forfeit **100% of the Study Deposit + 20% of RD2**
- **If physically infeasible:** Forfeit only **50% of the Study Deposit**
- **If cost estimate increased by >50% between Phase 1 and Phase 2** (excluding SDU costs): Forfeit only **100% of the Study Deposit** (no RD2 penalty)

**Security Forfeiture (Post-Acceptance):** If a project *accepts* cost allocation and posts Security but *later

steps.reasoning: The user is asking about the NYISO (New York Independent System Operator) generation interconnection cluster study process, including major phases, decision points, and associated fees. Let me search for this information.

steps.reasoning:
The search results provide comprehensive information about the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process. Let me also get more detail from the OATT Attachment HH document to ensure I have complete information about the additional SDU study and post-Phase 2 processes.

Let me compile information from the sources I have:

1. **Pre-Application Phase** (Optional, Rolling)
   - Heatmap review
   - Pre-Application Report ($5,000 per POI, max 2 POIs)
   - Pre-Application scoping meeting
   - Connecting Transmission Owner provides report within 25 business days of scoping meeting
   
2. **Application Window** (45 calendar days for standard; 75 for Transition Cluster)
   - Submit Interconnection Request or CRIS-Only Request with a non-refundable $10,000 application fee for full requests or $5,000 for CRIS-only studies
   - Study deposits vary by project size: $100,000 for projects under 80 MW, $150,000 for 80-200 MW, $250,000 for 200+ MW, or $50,000 for CRIS-only requests
   - NYISO validates completeness within 10 business days (15 for transition clusters), and the Interconnection Customer has 10 business days to address any deficiencies
   - Limited to one Point of Interconnection per request

3. **Customer Engagement Window** (70 calendar days standard; 90 for transition)
   - NYISO publishes the cluster member list within 10 business days, and participants can request POI changes within 5 business days of that publication before the Cluster Study Agreement is executed
   - The Connecting Transmission Owner performs a Physical Infeasibility Screen and a Group Scoping Meeting is held for all cluster members
   - At Decision Point 1, participants have 5 business days to either post Readiness Deposit 1 ($4,000/MW) to proceed to Phase 1 or withdraw with penalties ranging from 25% of the Study Deposit to $5,000 if physically infeasible, or no penalty if withdrawn within 5 business days of cluster member list posting

4. **Phase 1 Study** (180 calendar days standard)
   - The Connecting and Affected Transmission Owners identify the necessary facilities including Connecting Transmission Owner's Attachment Facilities, Distribution Upgrades, and Local System Upgrade Facilities, while also preparing groundwork for Phase 2 analyses
   - The Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report gets approved by the NYISO Operating Committee, then at Decision Point 2 participants have 10 business days to either post Readiness Deposit 2 (the greater of the initial deposit or 20% of Phase 1 cost estimates for Local SUF and CTOAF) to advance to Phase 2, or withdraw and face penalties of 50% of the Study Deposit plus 10% of RD1

, or 25% of the Study Deposit if physically infeasible

Phase 2 runs for 270 calendar days, during which NYISO conducts assessments using baseline and project-specific case studies to identify System Upgrade Facilities, Distribution Upgrades, and System Deliverability Upgrades, while Connecting and Affected Transmission Owners develop cost estimates and the analyses get updated as needed, culminating in NYISO Operating Committee approval of the Cluster Study Report and potentially triggering an Additional SDU Study if required.

The Final Decision Period involves multiple iterative 7-day windows where projects can accept their cost allocation by paying cash or posting security equal to 100% of identified upgrade costs, or withdraw and face penalties ranging from 100% of the Study Deposit plus 20% of the Restudy Deposit up to 50% of the Security Deposit depending on feasibility and cost increases, with potential forfeiture of up to 100% of security if other projects depend on the upgrades.

After Phase 2 concludes, the project moves into Agreement Negotiation where the Interconnection Agreement and Construction Agreements for required upgrades are finalized, with the entire process taking approximately 569 days total.
